diagrams.description: This timeline traces major milestones of the Medieval period from the fall of the Western Roman Empire to the dawn of the early modern era. Key dates include: 476 CE fall of the West; 622 CE Hijra and rise of Islam; 800 CE Charlemagne crowned Emperor; 1066 Norman Conquest of England; 1095 first call for Crusade; 1215 Magna Carta; 1271 Marco Polo’s travels; the Black Death (1347‑1351); 1453 fall of Constantinople; and 1492 the end of Muslim Granada and Columbus’s voyage, marking the conventional close of the Middle Ages.
